speaker: Unknown
en: The days when you kind of knew what your career was going to be over, there were sickly some people were like, you want to be an architect, you want to be a software engineer, you want to be X or Y. I don't know.
zh: 在那些日子里，你大概知道你的职业生涯将会是什么，有一些病态的人比如，你想成为一名建筑师，你想成为一名软件工程师，你想成为 X 或 Y。我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the only kind of thinking I have here is that you slowly work out, there's a bunch of skills that you have, and there's a bunch of jobs that makes you good at, and then there's a bunch of stuff that people will pay you for.
zh: 我认为我这里唯一的想法是你慢慢弄清楚，你有一堆技能，有一堆工作让你擅长，还有一堆人们愿意为此付钱给你的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you want to get at least two of those, and preferably all three.
zh: 你想要至少得到其中的两个，最好三个都得到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So zooming out a little bit, let me ask you a Meta question.
zh: 所以稍微放大一点，让我问你一个 Meta 问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's a question about AI that you think nobody's asking yet, or not enough people are asking that we should be asking ourselves?
zh: 你认为有什么关于 AI 的问题是还没有人在问的，或者没有足够多的人在问，而我们自己应该问的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Sure.
zh: 当然。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, we talked about value capture.
zh: 我的意思是，我们谈到了价值获取。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously this is a whole, everyone is asking like, I'm not sure how many people are asking whether model labs have pricing power.
zh: 显然这完全是一个，每个人都在问，我不确定有多少人在问模型实验室是否有定价权。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think a lot of people are just presuming that the situation today will continue, or that of course they will.
zh: 我认为很多人只是假设今天的情况会继续，或者他们当然会。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think that's maybe a question that not enough people ask.
zh: 所以我认为这可能是一个没有足够多人问的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the question I pose towards the end of my presentation, which we talked about earlier, is like, what's the task and what's job?
zh: 我认为我在演示结束时提出的问题，我们之前谈过的，就像是，什么是任务，什么是工作？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or what is just the thing that becomes a button or make SKU versus what are people actually hiring you for?
zh: 或者什么是仅仅变成一个按钮或制造 SKU 的东西，相对于人们真正雇用你是为了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that kind of a useful way of thinking about this?
zh: 这是一种思考这个问题的有用方式吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And clearly there are going to be some jobs where no, that is just a task, and that job gets automated away.
zh: 显然会有一些工作，不，那只是一个任务，那个工作被自动化掉了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But there's a bunch where that kind of isn't the question.
zh: 但有一堆工作，那并不是问题所在。

speaker: Unknown
en: The way I actually pulled that together at the end of the deck was a chart of a global recorded music revenue, which as you may know is kind of a U-shaped curve more or less.
zh: 我实际上在幻灯片最后把它拼凑在一起的方式是全球录制音乐收入的图表，你可能知道这或多或少是一个 U 型曲线。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's dropped by about half from 2000 to 2015 or so, and since then has come back to about 75% of the peak, adjusted for inflation.
zh: 所以它从 2000 年到 2015 年左右下降了一半左右，从那时起又恢复到了峰值的 75% 左右，经通货膨胀调整。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the way that I look at this is to say, and that's driven by streaming.
zh: 我看待这个的方式是说，这是由流媒体驱动的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I kind of looked at this and said, well, the first half of this chart is saying, what happens if I don't have to pay $15 to get a CD to get that track?
zh: 我看这个并说，好吧，这张图表的前半部分是说，如果我不必支付 15 美元买一张 CD 来获得那首曲目会发生什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And the second half of the chart is saying, what happens if $15 a month gets you all the music that there is?
zh: 图表的后半部分是说，如果每月 15 美元让你获得所有的音乐会发生什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's kind of a completely different sort of question.
zh: 所以这是一种完全不同的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's a way that you could look at Google, or the way you could look at Airbnb or all these kinds of companies.
zh: 这是一种你可以看待 Google 的方式，或者你可以看待 Airbnb 的方式，或者所有这类公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it to begin with you do the old thing but more whether you need technology, you do the old thing, but more of it on the new place.
zh: 一开始是你用技术做旧事但更多，你需要技术，你做旧事，但在新地方做更多。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you put Flickable mobile, you print out Googles, and then you make new things that are only possible with a new thing.
zh: 所以你把 Flickable 放在移动端，你打印出 Googles，然后你制造只有新事物才可能的新事物。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then maybe you go a bit further and you kind of completely redefine the question and you make something that isn't that at all.
zh: 然后也许你走得再远一点，你完全重新定义了这个问题，你制造了一些根本不是那样的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Spotify is not an online music store.
zh: Spotify 不是一个在线音乐商店。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's something else.
zh: 它是别的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And right now, those questions, you only even know what the question is after it's been asked and you've built a billion dollar thing that lots of people use because obviously Spotify look crazy, and people look crazy, and Airbnb look crazy.
zh: 现在，这些问题，你甚至知道问题是什么只有在它被问出之后，并且你已经建立了一个数十亿美元的东西，很多人在使用，因为显然 Spotify 看起来很疯狂，人们看起来很疯狂，Airbnb 看起来很疯狂。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that's, I think, the way to get at what this means, is you have to get past, we do the old stuff but more, and you have to get to what do you do that's different that's because of this.
zh: 但这，我认为，是理解这意味着什么的方式，是你必须超越，我们做旧事但更多，你必须达到因为这个而做的不同的事情是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: What has this change?
zh: 这改变了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What wasn't possible before?
zh: 以前什么是不可能的？

speaker: Unknown
en: What gets unlocked as opposed to just doing the old thing but more of it?
zh: 什么被解锁了，而不仅仅是做旧事但做得更多？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just to support this kind of general theme you have of it's like we don't know what is going to happen, this is unprecedented, if you're to zoom out a few years ago, maybe three years ago, four years ago, the last profession you think would be automated is engineering and coding.
zh: 只是为了支持你这种总体主题，就像我们不知道会发生什么，这是前所未有的，如果你要放大到几年前，也许是三年前，四年前，你认为最后会被自动化的职业是工程和编码。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, that feels like the hardest thing that's like, we're going to need people to build these things.
zh: 就像是，那感觉是最难的事情，就像是，我们需要人来构建这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now it's like the most transformed role of any role.
zh: 现在它似乎是任何角色中最转变的角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: You went from writing all your codes to 0% of your code is AI.
zh: 你从写下你所有的代码到你的代码 0% 是 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's almost like you didn't realize it was boring manual labor that could be automated.
zh: 就像你没有意识到它是可以自动化的无聊的手工劳动。

speaker: Unknown
en: You thought it was something else.
zh: 你以为它是别的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's funny.
zh: 很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I was looking at this, this whole, there's this sort of US government called data set called ONET or something like that, which tries to analyze every single job and then people try and kind of score it, and they try and say, "Well, this profession is X or Y percent exposed to AI, and AI can do Z percent of it today."
zh: 我的意思是，我在看这个，这整个，有一种美国政府的叫作数据集叫作 ONET 或类似的东西，它试图分析每一个工作，然后人们尝试给它打分，他们试图说，「好吧，这个职业有 X 或 Y 百分比暴露于 AI，AI 今天可以做 Z 百分比。」

speaker: Unknown
en: I think this is just the most ridiculous bunch of diluted horseshit.
zh: 我认为这只是一堆最荒谬的稀释的废话。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's two reasons for this.
zh: 这有两个原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first reason is that this is, ironically, this is the logical systems problem, the expert system's problem.
zh: 第一个原因是，讽刺的是，这是逻辑系统问题，专家系统的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: The problem with expert systems is, for anyone who doesn't know, you try to recognize a picture of a cat and so you start building up logical steps.
zh: 专家系统的问题是，对于任何不知道的人，你试图识别一张猫的图片，所以你开始建立逻辑步骤。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you make an edge detector, and then you make a third detector, and you make an eye detector and you make an ear detector, and 15 years later you've got 700 steps and it doesn't work.
zh: 所以你制造了一个边缘检测器，然后你制造了一个第三个检测器，你制造了一个眼睛检测器，你制造了一个耳朵检测器，15 年后你有 700 个步骤，但它不工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is what happens when you try and look at a profession and sort of break it down by which bits can be automated and which can't.
zh: 这就是当你试着审视一个职业并试图将其拆解为哪些部分可以自动化、哪些不能时会发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't describe a profession like that.
zh: 你不能那样描述一个职业。

speaker: Unknown
en: But anyway, we can't.
zh: 但无论如何，我们不能。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't look at a senior partner at a law firm and say, "Well, 17% of their work could be automated."
zh: 你不能看着一家律师事务所的高级合伙人说，“嗯，他们 17% 的工作可以被自动化。”

speaker: Unknown
en: This is horseshit.
zh: 这是胡扯。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't do that.
zh: 你不能那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the other side of the fallacy though is to talk about taxi drivers.
zh: 我认为这个谬误的另一面是谈论出租车司机。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, if we'd been having this conversation in 1997, it's like the Uber test.
zh: 所以，如果我们在 1997 年进行这场对话，这就像 Uber 测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: Imagine we're in 1997, what will be crushed by the internet?
zh: 想象我们在 1997 年，什么会被互联网粉碎？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, newspapers will be fine because they'll save money on the printing bills.
zh: 嗯，报纸会没事，因为它们会节省印刷费用。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is like a jake, but people said that.
zh: 这就像是个笑话，但人们确实那样说过。

speaker: Unknown
en: Newspaper, the internet will be great for newspapers.
zh: 报纸，互联网对报纸来说会很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Their printing bills will get down.
zh: 它们的印刷账单会下降。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, yes, but no.
zh: 嗯，是的，也不全是。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the other side is, well, obviously our taxi drivers, you couldn't automate that with the internet.
zh: 但另一方面是，嗯，显然我们的出租车司机，你无法用互联网自动化那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's got nothing to do with the internet.
zh: 那跟互联网毫无关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe you'd have internet booking, but like, no, that's not going to change anything.
zh: 也许你会有互联网预订，但是就像，不，那不会改变任何事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course, it completely changes the whole thing.
zh: 而且当然，它完全改变了整个事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the example I saw the other day was things that won't be affected by AI personal trainers.
zh: 所以我前几天看到的一个例子是不会受 AI 影响的事情，私人教练。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I take my iPhone and I balance it on the metal piece with the camera pointed at me, and I ask an AI to build me a training routine and watch me and tell me if I'm doing it right.
zh: 我拿出我的 iPhone，把它架在那个金属片上，摄像头对着我，我让一个 AI 为我建立一个训练常规，并看着我，告诉我我做得是否正确。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why do I need a personal trainer?
zh: 为什么我需要一个私人教练？

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, that might be complete nonsense, but that's how these things work.
zh: 现在，这可能完全是废话，但这就是这些事情运作的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: The stuff that you don't think is, you can't predict which things are going to be exposed necessarily.
zh: 那些你不认为会被（影响）的东西，你无法预测哪些事物必然会被暴露。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or a lot of the big companies are things that didn't look like that would work and didn't look like I was exposed.
zh: 或者许多大公司是那些看起来不像是行得通、也不像是我会被暴露的事物。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other side of this, of course, is this is one of the charts at the end of my presentation is comparing Uber and Airbnb, because this is like the cliche from Mark Andreessen that Uber doesn't sell software to taxi companies, Airbnb doesn't sell software to hotels.
zh: 当然，这另一面是，这是我演讲结束时的一张图表，比较了 Uber 和 Airbnb，因为这就是 Marc Andreessen 的陈词滥调，Uber 不向出租车公司出售软件，Airbnb 不向酒店出售软件。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, now let's go and look at the market impact.
zh: 好的，现在让我们去看看市场影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, a whole bunch of cities were Uber demolished taxi business, and made it much bigger as well.
zh: 嗯，在许多城市 Uber 摧毁了出租车业务，但也让它变得更大了。

speaker: Unknown
en: The TAM became much bigger and everyone switched.
zh: TAM 变得大得多，每个人都转投了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Airbnb's impact hotels, if you actually go and look at the numbers, it's pretty marginal.
zh: Airbnb 对酒店的影响，如果你真的去看看数据，那是相当微小的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They carved out this whole other business, and maybe they slowed down the growth of hotels a bit, but my wife flies to Milwaukee next week.
zh: 它们开辟了这整个其他业务，也许它们稍微减缓了酒店的增长，但我妻子下周要飞去米尔沃基。

speaker: Unknown
en: She's going to land at eight o'clock at night.
zh: 她将在晚上八点落地。

speaker: Unknown
en: She wants to go to a hotel, she wants to have a room service, she needs a bath, she needs a gym at 6:00 in the morning, and then 7:00 in the morning, she's going to drive to the client's site.
zh: 她想去一家酒店，她想要客房服务，她需要浴缸，她需要在早上六点去健身房，然后在早上七点，她要开车去客户的现场。

speaker: Unknown
en: She's not going to stay in Airbnb.
zh: 她不会住在 Airbnb。

speaker: Unknown
en: Absolutely zero chance she's going to stay in an Airbnb.
zh: 她住在 Airbnb 的机会绝对为零。

speaker: Unknown
en: And half of the hotel business is business travel.
zh: 而且酒店业务的一半是商务旅行。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as soon as you actually get into anything, then it gets complicated.
zh: 一旦你真正深入任何事物，事情就会变得复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: I remember somebody on social media said the problem with Benedict is his answer to everything is it depends.
zh: 我记得社交媒体上的某个人说 Benedict 的问题在于他对所有事情的回答都是视情况而定。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, yeah, it does.
zh: 就像是，是啊，确实是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It depends.
zh: 视情况而定。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, it's back to my 1997 point.
zh: 所以，这回到我 1997 年的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can say some of this, but you have to have that humility.
zh: 你可以说其中的一些，但你必须保持那种谦逊。

speaker: Unknown
en: Coming back to this phrase you use, presume radical uncertainty is a nice core thesis here.
zh: 回到你用的这个短语，假设存在根本的不确定性是一个很好的核心论点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So knowing all this, just it's hard to tell.
zh: 所以知道这一切，只是很难说。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't know exactly where it's going.
zh: 我们不知道它确切地会去哪里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Things are going to change a lot, but it'll probably be okay broadly.
zh: 事情会发生很大变化，但总的来说可能会没事的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just a lot of people listening are pretty worried about their jobs and their careers, and how much the world changes.
zh: 只是很多听众都很担心他们的工作和职业，以及世界会改变多少。

speaker: Unknown
en: What would be a couple things you recommend people do knowing what you know to be more successful in this future?
zh: 基于你所知道的，你会推荐人们做哪几件事以便在这个未来中更成功？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I should just wind back on what you just said.
zh: 嗯，我应该先回溯你刚才说的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, as Kim tells us, in the long run, we're all dead.
zh: 就像是，正如 Kim 告诉我们的，从长远来看，我们都死了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So on average, nobody died in World War I.
zh: 所以平均而言，第一次世界大战中没有人死亡。

speaker: Unknown
en: Great.
zh: 很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you're a 19-year-old in 1914, you've got a one in three chances of not coming back.
zh: 但如果你是 1914 年的一名 19 岁青年，你有三分之一的几率回不来。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yes, clearly there's a bunch of professions where this is a major question and particularly if you're an associate, or would've been thinking about being an associate, this is a major question.
zh: 所以是的，显然有一大批职业面临这是一个重大问题，特别是如果你是一名律师助理，或者本在考虑成为一名律师助理，这是一个重大问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's very unclear how those professions are going to play out.
zh: 而且这些职业将如何发展非常不明朗。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very unclear what happens to the pyramid structure of professional services.
zh: 专业服务的金字塔结构会发生什么也非常不明朗。

speaker: Unknown
en: The only answer I think one can have is, don't stick your head in the sand and say, "I hate all of this stuff," because that gives you a great feeling of moral superiority.
zh: 我认为一个人只能有的唯一答案是，不要把头埋在沙子里说，“我讨厌这一切”，因为这会给你一种极大的道德优越感。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you can go on Bluesky and shout at everybody, chat at each other about how evil AI is like.
zh: 你可以去 Bluesky 上对着大家大喊大叫，互相聊天说 AI 有多邪恶之类的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Great, I'm happy for you, but that's not going to help.
zh: 很好，我为你高兴，但这没有帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: What helps is you diving into this completely submerging yourself in it and coming at understanding what you can do with it, how this changes things, how you can be a great hire.
zh: 有帮助的是你全身心投入其中，完全沉浸其中，并开始了解你能用它做什么，这会如何改变事物，你如何成为一名优秀的雇员。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that may still not help.
zh: 但这可能仍然没有帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you're going to a law firm and they're like, well, we hired a hundred associates last year and this year we're only going to hire 50, go into the interview and say, "Well, I think AI is and I'm never going to use it," is probably not the right mood.
zh: 但是如果你去一家律师事务所，他们比如，嗯，我们去年招了一百名律师助理，今年我们只打算招五十名，你去面试时说，“嗯，我认为 AI 是……而且我永远不会使用它，”这可能不是正确的心境。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that may not be particularly comforting, but I don't think there's an alternative, is you have to dive into this and absorb it, and internalize it and think about what it means, just as you and I did with mobile and with the internet.
zh: 所以，这可能不是特别令人宽慰，但我认为没有别的选择，就是你必须潜入其中，吸收它，内化它，并思考它意味着什么，就像你我和手机以及互联网所做的那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that is actually very actionable and very consistent advice on the podcast, is just, do stuff, build it, don't just sit around and pontificate and be pissed at what's happening.
zh: 我认为这实际上是播客上非常可操作且非常一致的建议，就是，做点事情，构建它，不要只是闲坐着高谈阔论，并对正在发生的事情感到愤怒。

speaker: Unknown
en: To close this out, I'm going to take us to AI Corner, a recurring corner of the podcast.
zh: 为了结束这部分，我将带我们来到 AI 角落，这是播客的一个常设环节。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the question to you is just, what's one way you've used AI and use AI in your work or life that is really interesting, something that other people might be inspired by?
zh: 给你的问题是，你在你的工作或生活中使用 AI 和使用 AI 的一种方式是什么，那真的很有趣，其他人可能会从中受到启发的东西是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I struggle with this question, because I'm thought of the lawyer looking at ChatGPT.

en: And that's not a criticism, it's just an observation.

zh: 我想要机器为我做的那类事情，是 AI 目前非常非常非常不能为我做好的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I use it for proofreading, I use it for images, I used it redecorating my apartment, that worked fantastic, you're aware at that.
zh: 我用它来校对，我用它来处理图像，我用它重新装修我的公寓，效果很棒，你知道那件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's a picture of this room, repaint it at this light and this table and this rug.
zh: “这是这个房间的照片，在这个光线下、这张桌子和这张地毯的条件下重新粉刷它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: No change color of the rug.
zh: 不，改变地毯的颜色。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a kind of class of stuff where it works.
zh: 有一类事情它是有效的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I mean, a couple of years ago, somebody said AI is good at stuff that computers are bad at and bad at stuff that computers are good at.
zh: 但我的意思是，几年前有人说 AI 擅长计算机不擅长的事情，而不擅长计算机擅长的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I struggle to find many examples of those where I need it.
zh: 而我很纠结在我需要它的地方找到很多这样的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then I'm kind of a unique, weird job.
zh: 但我的工作有点独特和奇怪。

speaker: Unknown
en: I sit at my desk all day trying to synthesize a whole bunch of other stuff into a whole bunch of new ideas.
zh: 我整天坐在桌前，试图将大量其他东西综合成大量新想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not particularly common way for people to spend their time.
zh: 这不是人们打发时间的特别常见的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I struggle to find AI use cases.
zh: 我很难找到 AI 用例。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am the accountant looking at the spreadsheet and thinking, "Well, that's very clever, and this is clearly going to completely transform everything, but I actually don't make spreadsheets every day."
zh: 我就是那个看着电子表格并思考，“嗯，那很聪明，这显然将彻底改变一切，但我实际上每天并不制作电子表格”的会计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: I went to a standup comedy show of Pete Holmes, I don't know if you know him.
zh: 我去看了 Pete Holmes 的单口喜剧表演，我不知道你是否知道他。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he made this joke that we want AI to clean the poop off the street and do all these hard things that nobody wants to do, but instead it's like, "Oh, let me help you write.
zh: 他开了个玩笑，说我们想让 AI 清理街道上的粪便并做所有没人想做这些困难的事情，但它却像是，“哦，让我帮你写作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me help you create imagery."
zh: 让我帮你创作图像。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like this bohemian.
zh: 它就像这个波西米亚人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, "No, I don't want to do all these ugly things.
zh: 就像是，“不，我不想做所有这些丑陋的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to be creative, make art."
zh: 我想发挥创意，制造艺术。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I mean, there's variations of all of this.
zh: 嗯，我的意思是，这一切都有各种变体。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like I don't want the AI to do the stuff I do for fun.
zh: 就像我不希望 AI 做我做着好玩的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want it to do the boring stuff that I don't do for fun.
zh: 我希望它做那些我不做着玩的无聊事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And finding that mesh.
zh: 并找到那种契合点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, joking apart, this is going to come back to my chatbot point, that the chatbot is a blank screen in a jagged edge.
zh: 我的意思是，撇开笑话不谈，这将回到我的聊天机器人观点，聊天机器人是一个带有锯齿状边缘的空白屏幕。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what am I supposed to do and what will work?
zh: 我应该做什么，什么会有效？

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's a big problem.
zh: 这是一个大问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the solution to that problem is to wrap it in use cases.
zh: 那个问题的解决方案是将它包裹在用例中。

speaker: Unknown
en: Part of it is also like AI just disappears.
zh: 其中一部分也像是 AI 只是消失了。

en: So most of what I write now I dictate.

speaker: Unknown

zh: 我像语音留言一样口述，然后它自动被转录。

en: Is that still AI or is that just voice recognition?
zh: 这还是 AI 吗，还是那只是语音识别？

speaker: Unknown
en: There's probably an LLM in there.
zh: 那里面可能有一个 LLM。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: So maybe that's AI.
zh: 所以也许那是 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, okay.
zh: 嗯，好吧。

en: So what?

en: At a certain point, it's just automation.
zh: 在某种程度上，那只是自动化。

en: What do you use for that, for voice transcription?

en: So, I actually find that Apple Notes, the app or the one built into the iPhone works fine.

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I'm conscious of the people want others, but I mean, I dictate it.
zh: 我的意思是，我意识到人们想要其他的，但我意思是，我用听写输入它。

speaker: Unknown
en: There it is.
zh: 就这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It worked.
zh: 它奏效了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I'm happy with that.
zh: 所以，我对那个很满意。

speaker: Unknown
en: All right.
zh: 好的。

en: Final question before we get to our very exciting lightning round.

zh: 还有什么其他你想分享的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything else you want to leave listeners with?
zh: 还有什么其他你想留给听众的吗？

speaker: Unknown

zh: 去读那个演示文稿，并订阅我的时事通讯，然后你将会获得更多份 Benedict Evans 的智慧，其中一些甚至可能是有用的。

en: Someone unsubscribed for my newsletter and they said, "You didn't give me any actionable stock ideas."
zh: 有人退订了我的时事通讯，他们说，“你没有给我任何可操作的股票点子。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "Well, on one level, that's completely true.
zh: 我就说，“嗯，在某种程度上，那是完全正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: On the other level, maybe not."
zh: 在另一个层面上，也许不是。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, with that, Benedict, we've reached our very exciting lightning round.
zh: 好的，既然这样，Benedict，我们到了非常激动人心的闪电回合。

zh: 你准备好了吗？

en: I sometimes often joke that there's a classic British comedy from the late 19th century called Three Men in a Boat, which is like my I Ching.
zh: 我有时经常开玩笑说有一部 19 世纪末的经典英国喜剧叫《三怪客泛舟记》，它就像我的《易经》。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're having trouble hanging a picture.

en: Well, there's a section about that.

speaker: Unknown
en: We're having trouble doing this.

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, well, there's a story about that, all of which are hilarious.

speaker: Unknown
en: So, when the meat packers of Chicago reach the point that it's cheaper to ship a cow from New York to Chicago, kill it, pack it, and then ship it back to New York than to kill it in New York, and the pricing of refrigerator cars, and it's exactly like reading about broadband.
zh: 所以，当芝加哥的肉类加工商达到这样的程度，把一头牛从纽约运到芝加哥，宰杀它，包装它，然后再运回纽约，比在纽约宰杀它更便宜，以及冷藏车的定价，这就像在读关于宽带的内容。

en: It's all the same kind of...

en: So those kinds of business issues, which is fascinating.

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.

en: I don't know.

en: I've dropped so badly off the current media treadmill, and I just spend most of my time watching classics, which are always the ones that you're supposed to have seen and that all seem intimidating.

speaker: Unknown
en: It was really interesting, and it's like it's only like an hour.

speaker: Unknown
en: So, go watch one of those movies that you are supposed to have seen, or hadn't seen.
zh: 所以，去看一部你应该看过但还没看过的电影吧。

en: I don't know.

en: I mean, it's partly a function of product ships, a platform chips.

speaker: Unknown
en: All the white space went for cool new apps, and now we haven't quite got... Actually, this is to the earlier point, we don't have breakout a consumer AI apps yet, but I think because of marginal cost more than anything else, you can't make it free and get 50 million users and then have a revenue bottle.
zh: 所有很酷的新应用程序的空白空间都消失了，现在我们还没有……实际上，这也是回到之前的点，我们还没有爆发性的消费者 AI 应用程序，但我认为主要是因为边际成本，你不能让它免费并获得 5000 万用户，然后拥有一个收入瓶颈。

speaker: Unknown
en: But we don't have those breakout things yet.
zh: 但我们还没有那些爆发性的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: For consumer.
zh: 面向消费者。

speaker: Unknown
en: For consumer, no.
zh: 面向消费者，没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: I keep getting these ads for voice recorders.
zh: 我一直收到这些语音记录器的广告。

speaker: Unknown
en: Somebody's selling a business card size hardware voice recorder.

speaker: Unknown
en: But I didn't get it.
zh: 但我没买。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've got the voice recorder on my phone.

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.

en: All kinds of cool stuff coming.

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.

en: I suppose both mentioned earlier, apparently, I mostly say it depends.

en: That's going to be the title.

en: It'll probably be okay.

en: Yeah.

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the vibe I get.

speaker: Unknown
en: I like that.

speaker: Unknown
en: I like that.
zh: 我喜欢那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's probably going to be okay.
zh: 大概会没事的。

en: Not for sure.

en: Okay.

en: It is, yes.

en: I mean, I was a telecoms analyst and mobile analyst and I kept all my phones up to a point.
zh: 我的意思是，我曾经是电信分析师和移动分析师，在一点之前我保留了我所有的手机。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now they're kind of uninteresting.
zh: 现在它们有点没意思了。

en: But as you may remember, before the iPhone, particularly outside the USA, there was this huge creativity and expansion in what phones looked like, because everyone was basically innovating around a little teeny, tiny gray square.
zh: 但你可能还记得，在 iPhone 之前，特别是美国以外，在手机外观方面有巨大的创造力和扩张，因为每个人基本上都在创新一个小小的灰色方块。

speaker: Unknown
en: So everyone was trying to differentiate from everything else, before it kind of resulted.
zh: 所以每个人都试图从其他一切中脱颖而出，在它最终定型之前。

en: Everything converged on one shape.

en: Okay.

en: Okay.

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not so crazy.

speaker: Unknown
en: So I came back from Japan in 2001 and my phone had a color screen and a camera.
zh: 所以 2001 年我从日本回来，我的手机有彩屏和摄像头。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just had endless client meetings and people just wanted to see the phone with a color screen.
zh: 然后我有无尽的客户会议，人们只是想看那部有彩屏的手机。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's mind-blowing.
zh: 这简直令人难以置信。

speaker: Unknown
en: Didn't work outside Japan.
zh: 在日本以外不能使用。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I plugged it in the other day.
zh: 所以，前几天我把它插上了电源。

speaker: Unknown
en: It still charges up.
zh: 它仍然能充电。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, clearly I can't do anything with it.
zh: 我的意思是，显然我拿它做不了任何事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, there's a little bit of an analogy in there as well in that we thought there'd be all these different shapes and sizes.
zh: 我的意思是，那里也有点类比，我们原以为会有所有这些不同的形状和尺寸。

speaker: Unknown
en: And before the iPhone, people kind of imagined, "Well, some people will have a little pocket PC, and some people have a keyboard, and you have folding..." There were all these different ideas for what it would look like.
zh: 在 iPhone 之前，人们有点想象，“好吧，有些人会拥有一个小型的 pocket PC，有些人会有键盘，你有折叠的……”有所有这些关于它外观的不同想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we didn't realize it was all going to converge on one device.
zh: 我们没有意识到它会收敛到一个设备上。

speaker: Unknown
en: Benedict, this was amazing.
zh: Benedict，这太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I learned a ton.
zh: 我学到了很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel better after this conversation.
zh: 这次谈话后我感觉好多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two final questions.
zh: 最后两个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where can folks find you online?
zh: 大家在网上哪里可以找到你？

speaker: Unknown
en: Where do they find this presentation?
zh: 他们在哪里可以找到这个演示文稿？

speaker: Unknown
en: And how can listeners be useful to you?
zh: 听众如何能对你有用？

speaker: Unknown
en: If you can Google me, as I always say, my parents had good SEO.
zh: 如果你 Google 我，正如我常说的，我父母的 SEO 不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, Google Benedict Evans.
zh: 所以，Google Benedict Evans。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so there's a website where I publish all the presentations that I've done.
zh: 有一个网站，我发布了我做过的所有演示文稿。

speaker: Unknown
en: And sign up for my newsletter, which comes out every week.
zh: 并注册我的每周通讯。

speaker: Unknown
en: Otherwise, how can they be useful to me?
zh: 否则，他们怎么能对我有用呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm always trying to understand stuff and I'm always trying to ask different questions.
zh: 我总是试图理解事物，我总是试图问不同的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: The worst thing in tech is to carry on talking about the same stuff.
zh: 科技界最糟糕的事情是继续谈论同样的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like the moment you really understand something, it's the moment you have to push onto something else.
zh: 就像当你真正理解某事的那一刻，就是你必须转向其他事情的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I'm always trying to think like, "No, am I just talking about the same thing over and over again?"
zh: 所以我总是试着这样想，“不，我是否只是一遍又一遍地谈论同一件事？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Last year I just spent probably too much time saying, "But these models still hallucinate.
zh: 去年我可能花了太多时间说，“但是这些模型仍然会产生幻觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: Stop telling me they don't hallucinate."
zh: 别告诉我它们不会产生幻觉。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And they do.
zh: 它们确实会。

speaker: Unknown
en: They still hallucinate.
zh: 它们仍然会产生幻觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: You push them, push them a little bit further, any question and you'll still get like, "No, that's not true," but that doesn't mean they're not useful.
zh: 你推动它们，把它们再推动一点，任何问题，你仍然会得到像，“不，那不是真的”这样的回答，但这并不意味着它们没有用。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have to keep pushing myself.
zh: 所以你必须不断推动我自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's always that the challenge for me, is how do I push?
zh: 所以这对我来说一直是个挑战，就是我该如何推动？

speaker: Unknown
en: And then yes, if you want me to come and present to your board in the Caribbean, then let me know.
zh: 然后是的，如果你想让你的董事会请我去加勒比海做演讲，那就告诉我。

speaker: Unknown
en: And by the way, the domain is bend-evans.com if folks who want to check you out.
zh: 顺便说一下，域名是 bend-evans.com，如果大家想访问你的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: And evans.com.
zh: 还有 evans.com。

speaker: Unknown
en: Benedict, thank you so much for being here.
zh: Benedict，非常感谢你来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thanks a lot.
zh: 多谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: Bye, everyone.
zh: 再见，大家。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you so much for listening.
zh: 非常感谢大家的收听。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you found this valuable, you can subscribe to the show on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or your favorite podcast app.
zh: 如果你觉得这很有价值，你可以在 Apple Podcasts、Spotify 或你喜欢的播客应用上订阅本节目。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, please consider giving us a rating or leaving a review, as that really helps other listeners find the podcast.
zh: 另外，请考虑给我们打分或留下评论，因为这真的能帮助其他听众找到这个播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can find all past episodes or learn more about the show at lennyspodcast.com.
zh: 你可以在 lennyspodcast.com 找到往期节目或了解更多关于本节目的信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: See you in the next episode.
zh: 下一集见。
